div.head: VISIT REPORT
ICL Slough
CUA CAFS SIG WP
9 May 1985
div.p: Prioritisation of enhancement proposals occupied most of the morning. Of the thirty odd topics previously categorised as requiring discussion, the bulk concerned getting IDMS to use CAFS more often eg for sequential scans (the IDMS response to which was "probably useless in most commercial databases"); I was volunteered to spell out why this was probably not the case. Of the improvements to QM we particularly want, improvements to PRINT and better support for numerics both ranked high, but better performance statistics from the CAFS unit itself are apparently accessible only from 3900 machines. The PDS wangle for performing inequality joins also has to be written up.

After lunch there was much some waving of ecstatic press cuttings, and talk of forthcoming jamborees. Various high fliers are proposed for the next CAFS lunch, to be held in the Cafe Royal this winter, the names of R. Wilmot, a man from STC and even one J. Martin being mentioned as candidates for the guru slot on the bill.

I. Carpenter had been to the dry run of the new CAFS appreciation course described at the last meeting; he reported that it was pretty much as expected, i.e. lots of detail on how to use QM, with only token gestures to CSO and DCI, but still a good introduction, if an unusual mixture of design considerations and practicalities.

The rest of the day (a long one) was spent on the Report proper. It was agreed that OUCS typesetting was preferable to typing, but it would have to be on A4! Sections of the report in various stages of completion were discussed: several draft sections in various stages of inchoateness were tabled and politely shredded.
